instructions.content: Source the project's conventions from AGENTS.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, existing configs, code review history, and project language docs before mapping to tool rules.
Map each convention to the correct tool: naming/vue to ESLint, formatting to Prettier, types to tsconfig.json, with appropriate rule names and severity.
After generating config, run the verification commands (eslint, prettier --check, vue-tsc) to confirm the tooling works as expected.
NEVER add a tool the project doesn't already use without asking — introducing a new linter/formatter is a team decision.
NEVER change Prettier formatting options without verifying the change across the full codebase — formatting wars are expensive.
NEVER configure ESLint rules that conflict with Prettier rules — use eslint-config-prettier to disable style rules ESLint handles.
NEVER add a rule that produces noise — warnings that are always ignored reduce trust in the tool.
